data.description:
I owed a friend £22.91 for an Uber.

I transferred them £22.19 by accident.

They never brought it up.

Nobody would bring up 72p.

I did it on purpose. 

I've been doing it for years.

Every time I have to transfer someone money, I "accidentally" leave off a bit.

I call it the Plausible Deniability Typo.

£24.37 instead of £24.73.
£9.38 instead of £9.83.
£3.46 instead of £3.64.

Sometimes if I'm feeling really risky, I'll do £18.54 instead of £19.54.

They'd look like a complete tool to quibble over this.

Instead, they are a tool in a different way.

A tool for me to get to the top.

I've probably made £100 over 5 years. | 5,756 comments on LinkedIn

Ken Cheng’s Post

Ken Cheng
1y Edited
  • Report this post

I owed a friend £22.91 for an Uber. I transferred them £22.19 by accident. They never brought it up. Nobody would bring up 72p. I did it on purpose. I've been doing it for years. Every time I have to transfer someone money, I "accidentally" leave off a bit. I call it the Plausible Deniability Typo. £24.37 instead of £24.73. £9.38 instead of £9.83. £3.46 instead of £3.64. Sometimes if I'm feeling really risky, I'll do £18.54 instead of £19.54. They'd look like a complete tool to quibble over this. Instead, they are a tool in a different way. A tool for me to get to the top. I've probably made £100 over 5 years.

66,495 5,756 Comments
Like Comment
Emmanuel Ukponzene 1y
  • Report this comment

I'm just loving the comments on this post 🤣

Like
Reply
52 Reactions 53 Reactions
Joseph Millar 1y
  • Report this comment

How do i block stupid posts like this

Like
Reply
57 Reactions 58 Reactions
Beau Glitschka 1y
  • Report this comment

This post restored my faith in LinkedIn. Then I read the comments.

Like
Reply
83 Reactions 84 Reactions
Ryan Bernsten 1y
  • Report this comment

Literally obsessed with the outrage given to a satirical post designed to provoke outrage. Like watching people scream at an article, not realizing it's The Onion. Save some outrage for the things that matter, LinkedIn Warriors!

Like
Reply
212 Reactions 213 Reactions
Wanda-Karen Muellner-Plenteda 1y
  • Report this comment

Nothing to be proud of in my opinion.

Like
Reply
12 Reactions 13 Reactions
Ryan Barrett 1y
  • Report this comment

Hundreds of replies and only a small portion understand that this is a joke. It’s funny because he is delivering terrible advice in the same fashion as other “grindset”/“hustle”-oriented creators, whose tips are generally also awful.

Like
Reply
226 Reactions 227 Reactions
Paul Falla 1y
  • Report this comment

I was doing almost the same. £24.73 instead of £24.37, £9.83 instead of £9.38, £3.64 instead of £3.46. Even £19.54 instead of £18.54. I’ve probably lost over £100 in the laat five years. Where am I going wrong?

Like
Reply
12 Reactions 13 Reactions
SALMAN SYED 1y
  • Report this comment

This isn’t funny at all. Taking small amounts from many people, knowing they might be too shy or hesitant to call it out, is not a ‘mistake’—it’s theft. And framing them as villains for speaking up only adds to the injustice. The Qur'an (Chapter 83) describes this as one of the major sins—profiting unfairly at the expense of others, even in small amounts, while they bear the loss. Let’s reflect on this and hold ourselves accountable.

Like
Reply
141 Reactions 142 Reactions
Sally Thomas 1y
  • Report this comment

Your friends now tell you the party is on the 21st when it’s actually on the 12th.

Like
Reply
43 Reactions 44 Reactions
Rui Cruz 1y
  • Report this comment

And you wonder why you're open to work, huh? Not doing it yourself, that's obvious, but publicizing that you do by copying and pasting stuff on your LinkedIn... At least you got a lot of engagement. There's no such thing as bad engagement, right? Right?...

Like
Reply
35 Reactions 36 Reactions
